title: Donanemab in early symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease: results from the TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 long-term extension
authors: J. Zimmer; J. Sims; C. Evans; E. S. M. Nery; Hong Wang; A. Wessels; Giulia Tronchin; Shoichiro Sato; L. L. Rakêt; Scott W. Andersen; C. Sapin; M. Paget; Ivelina Gueorguieva; P. Ardayfio; Rashna Khanna; D. Brooks; Brandy R. Matthews; Mark A. Mintun
journal: The Journal of Prevention of Alzheimer's Disease
abstract: Highlights • Donanemab-treated participants showed increasing clinical benefits over 3 years.• Clinical benefits of donanemab continued after the treatment course was completed.• Amyloid reaccumulation was comparable to the natural amyloid accumulation rate.• No new safety signals were seen versus the established safety profile of donanemab.
